data.content:
## Implementation Plan

### Root Cause

Radix UI Select component's SelectContent defaults to position="item-aligned" (src/components/ui/select.tsx:56). This positioning mode aligns the currently-selected item in the dropdown with the trigger element by manipulating viewport scroll position. In toolbar/filter contexts with scrollable parent containers, this causes visible page jitter when opening any Select dropdown.

In Queues View multi-select mode, BulkEditControls renders 4 Select components (Type, Priority, Profile, State) that all use this default. FilterControls has 3 more. Each one triggers viewport jitter on open.

The RepoSwitcher uses DropdownMenu (Radix DropdownMenu), which already uses Popper positioning by default — no jitter expected there.

### Fix

**Step 1: Change SelectContent default position to popper**
- File: src/components/ui/select.tsx
- Change position = "item-aligned" to position = "popper" in SelectContent (line 56)
- Popper positioning places dropdown below/above trigger using Floating UI without viewport manipulation
- All existing SelectContent consumers (filter-bar, beat-form, settings pages, pagination, retakes-view, move-to-project-dialog) are toolbar/form contexts where popper is appropriate
- No consumer currently passes position explicitly, so this single change fixes all

**Step 2: Add layout-stability test assertion**
- File: src/lib/__tests__/beats-page-layout.test.ts
- Add test asserting SelectContent uses position="popper" as default (source-level contract test matching existing pattern)
- Prevents regression if someone changes the default back

**Step 3: Browser verification**
- Open Queues View, select multiple beats to enter multi-select mode
- Open each of the 4 bulk-edit Select dropdowns and confirm no viewport shift
- Switch to FilterControls mode and verify filter dropdowns similarly stable
- Open RepoSwitcher DropdownMenu — confirm still works
- Use Chrome DevTools to capture scroll metrics before/after

### Complexity
Low. Single default value change in one shared component, plus a regression test.

### Risks
- Changing from item-aligned to popper alters dropdown visual placement globally (content appears below/above trigger instead of overlaying it). Standard UX, unlikely to cause issues, but worth visual-checking in settings pages and beat-form.

### Test Strategy
1. Source-level contract test — assert SelectContent defaults to popper position
2. In-browser manual verification — all pulldowns in Queues View multi-select mode
3. Run full test suite (bun run test:all) for regressions
4. Quality gates — lint, typecheck, build must pass
